channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Whiting Foundation names its 10 emerging authors of 2026
item.description: The annual Whiting Award for Emerging Writers comes with $50,000 to support each winner's work. It's one of the largest prizes granted to promising new authors.
item.content:encoded: The annual Whiting Award for Emerging Writers comes with $50,000 to support each winner's work. It's one of the largest prizes granted to promising new authors.
item.dc:creator: Ivy Buck

item.title: One man's mission to clean up South Florida's mangrove forests
item.description: Picking up trash in Mangrove forests has become a calling for one Florida man.
item.content:encoded: Picking up trash in Mangrove forests has become a calling for one Florida man.
item.dc:creator: Catherine Welch

item.title: Exploring the green side of Rio de Janeiro: a vast urban rain forest
item.description: Rio de Janeiro in Brazil is home to the world's largest urban rain forest. Woven among the city's bustling neighborhoods are soaring tree canopies, waterfalls, and miles of hiking trails.
item.content:encoded:
Rio de Janeiro in Brazil is home to the world's largest urban rain forest. Woven among the city's bustling neighborhoods are soaring tree canopies, waterfalls, and miles of hiking trails.

(Image credit: Brian Mann)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Actor Amanda Peet says she's 'cancer-free and extremely lucky'
item.description: Peet is always waiting for the other shoe to drop. Except last year there seemed to be three different shoes, as she faced her parents' deaths and a breast cancer diagnosis.
item.content:encoded:
Peet is always waiting for the other shoe to drop. Except last year there seemed to be three different shoes, as she faced her parents' deaths and a breast cancer diagnosis.

(Image credit: Richard Shotwell)
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Fela Kuti is the first African artist to enter the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
item.description: In December, the late Nigerian superstar became the first African musician to get a Grammy lifetime achievement award. Now he's making history as well at the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame.
item.content:encoded:
In December, the late Nigerian superstar became the first African musician to get a Grammy lifetime achievement award. Now he's making history as well at the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame.

(Image credit: Leni Sinclair/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Ian Brennan

item.title: No one likes being discombobulated. How did the feeling get such a fun name?
item.description: Sometimes you just need to recombobulate. That word isn't in the dictionary, but it is on a beloved sign at Milwaukee's airport.
item.content:encoded:
Sometimes you just need to recombobulate. That word isn't in the dictionary, but it is on a beloved sign at Milwaukee's airport.

(Image credit: Leo Patrizi)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Raffle winner thrilled to claim a $1 million Picasso with a $117 ticket
item.description: "How do I check that it's not a hoax?" said Ari Hodara. The Parisian art enthusiast could not believe his luck when he found out he'd won a Pablo Picasso painting worth $1 million.
item.content:encoded:
"How do I check that it's not a hoax?" said Ari Hodara. The Parisian art enthusiast could not believe his luck when he found out he'd won a Pablo Picasso painting worth $1 million.

(Image credit: Michel Euler)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Millions of people are pretending to be AI chatbots — for fun
item.description: Websites like youraislopbores.me have become playgrounds for people looking for light relief in a bot-heavy world.
item.content:encoded:
Websites like youraislopbores.me have become playgrounds for people looking for light relief in a bot-heavy world.

(Image credit: Screenshot by NPR)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: The labor economics of 'Alien' — and its lessons for inequality on Earth
item.description: Behind the acid blood and jump scares of the Alien franchise is an even more insidious horror: a single employer with unchecked power. How Weyland-Yutani helps explain monopsony — and the rise of inequality on Earth.
item.content:encoded: Behind the acid blood and jump scares of the Alien franchise is an even more insidious horror: a single employer with unchecked power. How Weyland-Yutani helps explain monopsony — and the rise of inequality on Earth.
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: Have we been reading Toni Morrison all wrong?
item.description: Harvard professor Namwali Serpell has been teaching Morrison for nearly two decades. Her book, On Morrison is a deep dive into the Nobel winner's complete body of work — 11 novels, plays and criticism.
item.content:encoded: Harvard professor Namwali Serpell has been teaching Morrison for nearly two decades. Her book, On Morrison is a deep dive into the Nobel winner's complete body of work — 11 novels, plays and criticism.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley
